title: Geometric and Numerical Approaches to KAM Theory
speaker: Rafael de la Llave
abstract: We review some recent developments in KAM theory.

proof_stages.label: Parametrized invariant-torus equation
proof_stages.summary: The talk replaces ambient changes of variables by a torus embedding K satisfying the invariance equation f∘K = K∘T_ω, so quasi-periodic KAM objects become zeros of a functional equation in fewer variables.

proof_stages.label: A posteriori Newton-KAM framework
proof_stages.summary: An approximate invariant torus with small residual, together with computable nondegeneracy/condition numbers and Diophantine and regularity hypotheses, is set up as input for a Newton or Nash-Moser iteration yielding a nearby true solution.

proof_stages.label: Conformally symplectic KAM theorem and consequences
proof_stages.summary: For families of maps that multiply the symplectic form by a constant, with suitable adjustable parameters, the a posteriori theorem is formulated and used to obtain existence, local uniqueness, regularity bootstrap, Lindstedt series, smooth parameter dependence, numerical algorithms, and breakdown criteria.

proof_stages.label: Automatic reducibility from symplectic geometry
proof_stages.summary: The linearized Newton equation is expressed in a moving frame built from DK and its symplectic conjugate, where symplectic cancellations reduce the correction equation to constant-coefficient cohomological equations solvable by Fourier methods.

proof_stages.label: Fast numerical realization and continuation to breakdown
proof_stages.summary: The reducible Newton step is implemented with FFT-friendly operations, enabling efficient continuation of invariant tori to the boundary of existence and numerical diagnostics for analytic/Sobolev blow-up, nondegeneracy failure, and loss of hyperbolicity.

proof_stages.label: Nontwist-torus geometry via counterterm potentials
proof_stages.summary: For symplectic families that fail twist or Rüssmann nondegeneracy, invariant tori are studied with extra parameters and counterterms, and in the integrable model these counterterms form the gradient of a potential whose critical and degenerate critical points encode invariant and nontwist tori.

proof_stages.label: Persistence theorem for nontwist singularities
proof_stages.summary: The parameterization/Newton scheme is lifted to families of Lagrangian tori, using Lagrangian potential theory and automatic reducibility to show that stable singularity-theoretic unfoldings persist under perturbation with explicit a posteriori bounds.

examples.name: Nontwist tori appearing from chaotic-looking dynamics
examples.note: A later numerical example shows that the computed potential can detect the birth of nontwist tori even when the phase portrait itself looks chaotic.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker describes computing the potential in chaotic-looking dynamics, detecting a near-zero, adjusting the parameter, and finding nontwist tori appearing from it.

passes.reason: The hidden nontwist tori are born at a degenerate threshold and refute the visual impression that the dynamics is purely chaotic.
